meta.principle: Mochaccino: schema → data → view — architecture as data, every render is a derivative
meta.provenance: app-inventory audit (2026-06-25, both-registries + filesystem sweep, M4-local only) → David's live pruning pass. Verdicts are David's words, not inferred.
meta.cross_machine_caveat: This map (and therefore this data) is M4-local only. The fleet is 5 Tailscale machines; the other machines need their own scan before the map is trustworthy (open question #3 in the source doc).

build_constraints.statement: Build it through the factory, not by hand. Bricks are built via the dark factory's own loop (daily operating model: BA agent → Marshall → Swagger → build-to-spec/SPAT), not ad-hoc hand-coding. The factory builds the factory.

The KBDE Extension SDK is a pending handover, not researchable — David delivers that knowledge; research is no substitute (the SDK isn't finished). Any Layer-2 surface / KBDE extension is BLOCKED until that handover lands. Layer-1 work (a data source / MCP feed) is NOT blocked — it needs no SDK.

meta.layer_definitions_note: 'own-world' was added beyond the requested enum — David's explicit classification for ARP/ARP-mobile is 'NEITHER' (not a surface, not a data-plane feed, not the data cluster at all). 'parked' and 'dead' cover the map's other two verdict buckets faithfully as given.
meta.channels_taxonomy_status: under-review — working model, not gospel (David, 2026-06-25). Authoritative rules: kybernesis/KBDE-KyberAgent-Enterprise/.claude/skills/extension-sdk/references/capability-model.md (committed). Plain-language: .../context/extension-sdk-semantic-meaning.md (gitignored, this-machine-only).

apps.name: The Inventory / Registries
apps.purpose: "What apps exist, do they run, what data they host" — the first data feed
apps.status_detail: exists, stale & incomplete (Kybernesis + several off-registry apps missing)
apps.verdict_notes: The realization that anchors the whole map: the registry/inventory IS the first data artifact in the bottom layer, not an app in its own right. Open question in the source doc: is 'inventory as data artifact' an extension, a data-plane feed, or its own thing?

apps.name: OMI Ingester
apps.purpose: Wearable transcript pipeline → great data for brains (dual-layer: also a surface, see surface-utility)
apps.verdict_notes: Dual-layer app — Layer-1 source AND Layer-2 surface. As a surface: 'cool app idea, definite extension — definite KBDE extension.'

apps.name: Cortex
apps.purpose: Knowledge-graph / second-brain store for agents — where brain-related stuff lives
apps.verdict_notes: Not a build target; keep listed as the brain store.

apps.name: Per-project Git / Mission State
apps.purpose: "Where are we up to" — commits, tickets, mission.md

apps.name: Blackboard MCP
apps.purpose: Shared K/V so dynamic workflows share data with each other
apps.verdict_notes: Important data pattern for dark-factory.

apps.name: appyradar-sentinel (new 'e')
apps.verdict_notes: The keep. Migrate the running sentinel from appyradar-sentinal (old 'a') to this one (open question #4 in the source doc).

apps.name: appyradar-sentinal (old 'a')
apps.purpose: Same role as appyradar-sentinel — fleet collector
apps.verdict_notes: Legacy — migrate to appyradar-sentinel ('e'). Repo trap: appysentinal→appysentinel was a RENAME (redirect); appyradar-sentinel is a NEW re-scaffold, NOT the same lineage as this old one.

apps.name: Switchboard
apps.purpose: Headless data plane (SSE + MCP) — process/session snapshots
apps.verdict_notes: Data-plane candidate. One of the four sister projects (Switchboard, Watchtower, AngelEye, AppyRadar Sentinel).

apps.verdict_notes: SS world.

apps.name: Mochaccino / Mocha-Census
apps.purpose: UI/UX mockups + rate-and-label board — where surfaces get mocked before they're built
apps.verdict_notes: Where POCs + visual documentation live — prototype a KBDE extension surface here first. This design (10-constellation) is itself an artifact of this layer.

apps.name: KBDE / KyberAgent-Enterprise
apps.purpose: The host — Electron app + daemon + Extension SDK seam; surfaces mount as sandboxed extensions across typed channels
apps.verdict_notes: The answer to "where do I see it." Extension SDK seam taxonomy is under active review (see meta.channels_taxonomy_status).

apps.name: AWB Gen 3
apps.verdict_notes: Listed deliberately alongside Gen 1 and Gen 2.

apps.name: AWB Gen 2
apps.verdict_notes: Also the 'prompt' member of the SupportSignal Suite (see supportsignal-suite).

apps.name: AWB Gen 1
apps.verdict_notes: Listed deliberately alongside Gen 2 and Gen 3.

apps.name: POEM OS
apps.verdict_notes: Keep.

apps.name: appydave-tools
apps.purpose: CLI tools (existing machinery that feeds/uses the layers)
apps.verdict_notes: Keep.

apps.name: FliVideo Suite
apps.purpose: "Just tools for my business, nothing more"
apps.verdict_notes: Own world, NOT the data cluster (maybe-someday extensions).

apps.name: SupportSignal Suite
apps.verdict_notes: Own world, NOT the data cluster (maybe-someday extensions). 'prompt' member = AWB Gen 2 (see awb-gen2).

apps.name: ARP (Agent Relationship Protocol)
apps.purpose: Agent-to-agent trust/identity/permissioning protocol + runtime (sovereign DIDs, per-purpose Connection Tokens, Cedar policy + consent, DIDComm transport, tamper-evident audit)
apps.status_detail: active/mature but paused — 159 commits, last May 3 2026. Hosted at arp.run. 8 apps / 22 pkgs.
apps.verdict_notes: Classification: NEITHER — not a surface or a data-plane feed. It's the Kybernesis agent-trust layer (KyberBot/KyberAgent consume it). One thread to keep: ARP's tamper-evident audit log is a plausible future data source — unverified whether it exposes an MCP/queryable feed.

apps.name: Kybernesis Satellites
apps.purpose: Kybernesis satellite apps, not yet scoped

apps.name: AppyCtrl
apps.verdict_notes: Superseded by KBDE.
